compiled_state.title: Pre-edit hook blocks Edit tool from orchestrator worktree when targeting main-repo worktree paths
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: The pre-bash.sh Edit tool hook blocks edits targeting files under /Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/.claude/worktrees/<agent>/ when the orchestrator session is in a different worktree (e.g., worktree-20260411-145909). The hook interprets all non-matching CWD paths as 'main repo edits', but agent worktrees under .claude/worktrees/ are not the main repo — they are isolated working directories. This causes Edit tool calls to be blocked with 'BLOCKED: Edit targeting main repo from worktree session' even when the orchestrator legitimately needs to touch those agent worktree files (e.g., adding REVIEW-DEFENSE comments, fixing table formatting). Workaround: dispatch a sub-agent with no isolation, or use Python3 via Bash. The hook should distinguish .claude/worktrees/<agent>/ paths from the actual main repo root.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral cluster (shared root cause), Score: 2 (BASIC). Root cause: hook_worktree_edit_guard does not allowlist REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/ paths, causing false-positive blocking when orchestrator edits sub-agent worktree files. Fix: add allowlist check before the main-repo block in hook_worktree_edit_guard.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present (or pre-loaded triage); intent-aligned assumed. Classification: behavioral cluster, Score: 2 (BASIC). Fix applied: allowlist MAIN_REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/ paths in hook_worktree_edit_guard (pre-bash-functions.sh). Tests: test-worktree-guard-agent-worktree-allowlist.sh (8 pass), test-worktree-agent-paths.sh (6 pass). Anti-pattern scan: no other occurrences found. FIX_RESULT: resolved
comments.author: Test
